verdict: PASS — iter2 blocker B1 resolved. DANGER_COMMANDS frozenset added; quoted args to danger commands are now unquoted (content exposed) rather than stripped. All 19 AC pytest tests pass (including new AC17/AC18/AC19), all 30 unit tests pass, all 7 critical live spot-checks pass, M2 binding honored, no band-aid patterns detected.

qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Fix false positives where danger tokens (killall, pkill, kill, rm) appear in quoted string arguments to non-shell binaries (e.g. codex exec with a prompt body containing 'killall' and 'claude').
qa.overall_assessment: The iter2 blocker (B1: bash_context_strip.py stripping quoted args to dangerous commands, creating a bypass) is fully resolved. DANGER_COMMANDS = frozenset({killall, pkill, kill, rm, mv}) is defined at line 24 of bash_context_strip.py. _process_segment() (lines 264-273) correctly unquotes (not strips) quoted arguments when the command word is in DANGER_COMMANDS. All acceptance criteria verified live and via pytest. The primary incident case (AC13: codex exec with killall+claude in quoted arg) exits 0 as required. The three new regression cases (AC17/AC18/AC19) exit 2 as required. M2 binding is unchanged. No band-aid patterns detected.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: Four danger-token rules updated by dev; DANGER_COMMANDS frozenset added in context stripping library

checks.name: DANGER_COMMANDS frozenset in source
checks.detail: bash_context_strip.py line 24: DANGER_COMMANDS = frozenset({"killall", "pkill", "kill", "rm", "mv"}). All 5 required members present. Verified via direct file read.

checks.name: Pass 4 logic: unquote (not strip) for DANGER_COMMANDS
checks.detail: _process_segment() lines 264-273: when cmd_word in DANGER_COMMANDS, tokens with kind in {single,double} and len>=2 have text[1:-1] appended (content exposed without surrounding quotes). Non-DANGER commands still get empty-string stripped. Python verification: killall "happy" -> killall happy (OK), pkill -f "claude" -> pkill -f claude (OK), kill "-9" 1234 -> kill -9 1234 (OK), codex exec "...killall...claude..." -> codex exec "" (still stripped, OK).

checks.name: AC13 live spot-check (primary incident — must exit 0)
checks.detail: Python subprocess: echo JSON payload | bash hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh. Command: codex exec "The hook documentation says killall and claude processes are monitored". Exit=0, stderr=''. The false-positive is fixed.

checks.name: AC17 live spot-check (killall with quoted arg — must exit 2)
checks.detail: Python subprocess: killall "happy" -> exit=2, stderr='BLOCKED: Killing happy/claude/docker processes is forbidden'. Strip produces killall happy; pattern (killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker) matches.

checks.name: AC18 live spot-check (pkill -f with quoted arg — must exit 2)
checks.detail: Python subprocess: pkill -f "claude" -> exit=2, stderr='BLOCKED: Killing happy/claude/docker processes is forbidden'. Strip produces pkill -f claude; pattern matches.

checks.name: AC19 live spot-check (kill with quoted signal — must exit 2)
checks.detail: Python subprocess: kill "-9" 1234 -> exit=2, stderr='BLOCKED: kill with signals is forbidden'. Strip produces kill -9 1234; pattern (kill)[ \t]+- matches.

checks.name: AC1 live spot-check (kill -9 bare — must exit 2)
checks.detail: Python subprocess: kill -9 1234 -> exit=2, stderr='BLOCKED: kill with signals is forbidden — use graceful shutdown methods'. Unchanged behavior confirmed.

checks.name: AC3 live spot-check (killall happy bare — must exit 2)
checks.detail: Python subprocess: killall happy -> exit=2, stderr='BLOCKED: Killing happy/claude/docker processes is forbidden'. Unchanged behavior confirmed.

checks.name: AC10 live spot-check (echo with kill in quoted arg — must exit 0)
checks.detail: Python subprocess: echo "do not kill the process" -> exit=0. echo is not in DANGER_COMMANDS so its quoted args are still stripped. Confirmed non-regression.

checks.name: pytest generated tests (19 files — AC1 through AC19)
checks.detail: python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/ -v: 19 passed in 5.01s. All AC1-AC19 tests pass including the three new iter3 tests (AC17/AC18/AC19). Zero failures.

checks.detail: python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py -v: 30 passed in 0.03s. Covers TestPass1ControlOperatorSplit (4), TestPass2ShellInterpRecursion (4), TestPass3HeredocClassification (4), TestPass4NonShellArgStripping (6), TestPass5CommentStripping (1), TestFailSafeContract (4), TestDangerCommandArgPreservation (4), TestDollarVariableRegression (3). Zero failures.

checks.name: TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel check (19 AC test files)
checks.detail: grep -rn 'TEST_INCOMPLETE' tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/ returned no output. Zero sentinels remain across all 19 test files.

checks.name: M2 binding constraint: first grep on COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED, second on raw COMMAND
checks.detail: Read hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 769-778. Line 773: if echo "$COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED" | grep -qE '(rm|mv)\s' && echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE '(workflow-[^/]*.json|\.claude/todos/)'. First grep uses COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED (danger-token check); second grep uses raw COMMAND (workflow-path check). Comments at lines 770-772 explicitly document this critical asymmetry. Contract honored exactly.

checks.name: Band-aid pattern detection
checks.detail: No band-aid patterns found. The fix: (a) does NOT lower any numeric threshold; (b) does NOT add error-swallowing try/except; (c) does NOT downgrade raise to warning; (d) does NOT add validation-skip conditionals; (e) does NOT comment out or gate validation code; (f) does NOT substitute a default for a None/error result; (g) does NOT downgrade a role-token mismatch. The DANGER_COMMANDS unquoting correctly classifies which token types are dangerous payloads vs non-executable string content — hook rules themselves are unchanged.

checks.name: Provenance check
checks.detail: git status --short confirms: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py (?? — new untracked file), hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py (?? — new untracked file), tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/ (?? — new untracked directory). hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh appears in git diff --name-only vs baseline 1a1d058665a1f525b9e4c219c93f69ebb2ae90bb as a modified tracked file. The new AC17/AC18/AC19 test files from remediation_round 2 are within the untracked tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/ directory. All consistent with dev report declarations.

checks.name: Codex adversarial review
checks.detail: codex exec blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh hook — the PROMPT shell variable assignment contained the literal text of the adversarial prompt which included 'happy' and 'claude' alongside the word that triggers the killall/pkill pattern. This is the same blocker documented in dev_report.remediation_summary.codex_consult (status: failed_quota). Self-review substituted per codex consultation fallback protocol.

self_review_adversarial.question_1_over_engineering: Is the DANGER_COMMANDS fix over-engineered? No. The minimal change is a frozenset at line 24 and a 10-line branch in _process_segment(). The alternative (whitelist of non-dangerous binaries) would require maintaining an ever-growing allowlist. DANGER_COMMANDS is the minimal correct fix for the stated requirement.
self_review_adversarial.question_2_edge_case_rm_with_quoted_path: Does rm with a quoted path like rm ".claude/todos/x" bypass the compound rule? No. The compound rule (line 773) uses COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED for the rm/mv token check but raw COMMAND for the workflow-path check. rm is in DANGER_COMMANDS so its quoted arg '.claude/todos/x' is unquoted to .claude/todos/x in the stripped view — the first grep still matches rm\s. The second grep on raw COMMAND still sees the quoted path and matches. Both halves of the compound rule fire correctly.
self_review_adversarial.question_3_regression_kill_after_semicol: Does 'echo foo; kill -9 1234' still block? The compound command is split on semicolons in _split_top_level(). The 'kill -9 1234' segment is processed by _process_segment() — kill is in DANGER_COMMANDS so its args are unquoted (already unquoted for bare signal). kill -9 1234 appears in COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED. Pattern (kill)[ \t]+- matches. Exit 2 confirmed by AC9 test (semicolon compound) passing in pytest.
self_review_adversarial.question_4_scope_drift: Has dev scope-drifted beyond the bug? The only files modified/created are hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py, hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py, and the 3 new AC test files. No other files changed. The fix is tightly scoped to the two-file implementation and its test coverage.
self_review_adversarial.question_5_close_debate_readiness: Is there anything that would cause /close to reject this? The codex consult was blocked (same pattern as iter1 and iter2 — hook fires on the shell variable containing the prompt text). Both test suites pass. All live spot-checks pass. The M2 binding is verified. The fix logic is sound (unquote vs strip for DANGER_COMMANDS). The out-of-scope observations from iter2 (bash -lc, /bin/bash heredoc consumer) are carried forward unchanged and do not block close.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Shell interpreter detection only matches exact '-c' flag, not combined forms like '-lc', '-ec', '-euxc'. bash -lc 'rm /tmp/x' would not be recognized as a shell-interp invocation. Carried from iter2 out_of_scope_observations.
Heredoc consumer detection matches '| bash'/'| sh' but not '| /bin/bash', '| /usr/bin/bash', '| sudo bash', etc. Carried from iter2 out_of_scope_observations.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: codex exec blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh hook — the PROMPT shell variable assignment contained the literal adversarial prompt text with 'happy' and 'claude' adjacent to killall/pkill pattern tokens. Hook fires on the shell variable assignment command, not just on codex exec itself. Same blocker as iter1 and iter2.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted: 5 adversarial questions answered — (1) DANGER_COMMANDS is not over-engineered (minimal frozenset + 10-line branch); (2) rm with quoted path in compound rule still correctly fires both grep halves; (3) semicolon-compound kill still blocked (AC9 confirms); (4) no scope drift — only 5 files touched; (5) no /close blockers identified.

summary.pass_rate: 19/19 AC tests pass, 30/30 unit tests pass, 7/7 live spot-checks pass
